code:
proof fn phi_2_init2none_vacuous_on_empty<T: Copy>(arr: Array<Option<T>, 0>)
    requires
        arr.wf(),
        0usize <= usize::MAX,
    ensures
        arr.wf(),
        forall|index: int| 0 <= index < 0 ==> #[trigger] arr@[index].is_None(),
{
}
reason: init2none on a zero-length array vacuously satisfies the postcondition with no work — the N <= usize::MAX guard is always true, potentially masking edge cases

name: wf_only_checks_length
code:
proof fn phi_4_wf_only_checks_length(arr1: Array<usize, 5>, arr2: Array<usize, 5>)
    requires
        arr1.wf(),
        arr2.wf(),
        arr1.seq@ =~= arr2.seq@,
    ensures
        forall|i: int| 0 <= i < 5 ==> arr1@[i] == arr2@[i],
{
}
reason: wf() only checks seq@.len() == N — two Arrays with identical ghost seqs are indistinguishable at spec level even if their concrete ar fields differ entirely
